title: The Session of Christ
speaker: Pastor Tito Lyro
source_files: 05-12-24 -PM- The Sesson of Christ - 1 Peter 3_22Pastor Tito Lyro.txt

outline.title: Introduction: Why We Call It the "Session" of Christ
subpoints: "Session" means seating — the word is used in church government because when elders are seated together they are in session, ruling
The session of Christ: Jesus is currently enthroned in heaven, ruling over all creation for the church
1 Peter 3:22 addresses both the ascension and the session of Christ — a providential passage for Ascension Sunday

outline.title: What 1 Peter 3:22 Says
subpoints: "God" here refers to the Father — Peter attributes to Jesus divine characteristics as ruler of all things
The right hand is a position of authority and execution — Joseph at Pharaoh's right hand, Mordecai at the king's right hand
Jesus first went to heaven (the ascension), then is at the right hand of God (the session)
Heaven is a created space where God condescends to dwell — Jesus is there bodily as the God-man
Prior to being at the right hand: angels, authorities, and powers were made subject to Christ — at the cross and resurrection

outline.title: What Christ Accomplished in His Ascension
subpoints: The ascension declares Christ's victory over all His enemies — He is the conquering king on parade
Ephesians 4:7-8 quoting Psalm 68: "When He ascended on high, He led captivity captive and gave gifts to men"
As He ascended, He distributed gifts to the church — specifically the teaching offices (Ephesians 4:11-12)
Our union with Christ means we ascended with Him — we now dwell positionally in the heavenly places
The ascension foreshadows our future ascension and rule with Christ

outline.title: What Christ Is Doing in His Session
subpoints: Pouring out the Holy Spirit on His church (Acts 2:33)
Forgiving the sins of His people — the king on the throne granting forgiveness (Acts 5:31)
Making intercession for His people — Romans 8:34: "Christ who died... is even at the right hand of God, who also makes intercession for us"
Persevering the saints — Hebrews 12:2: looking to Jesus, the author and finisher of our faith, who sat down at the right hand of the throne
Sovereignly ruling all things for the good of His church

outline.title: How We Are to Live in Light of the Session of Christ
subpoints: We exercise spiritual authority to conduct spiritual warfare — tearing down strongholds, bringing every thought captive (2 Corinthians 10; Ephesians 6)
We have freedom from fearing demonic powers — resist the devil and he flees; we do not fear him because Christ is Lord even over him
We exercise dominion over the created order through the power of the gospel — making disciples of all nations under Christ's authority

summary:
In this Sunday evening sermon on 1 Peter 3:22, preached on Ascension Sunday, Pastor Tito Lyro explores the doctrine of the session of Christ — Jesus's ongoing ministry as He is enthroned and ruling at the right hand of the Father in heaven. He explains why this terminology is used in church government: when elders are seated together in governance they are "in session," and when Christ is seated on the throne He is ruling all creation for the church. The work of Christ did not end with the resurrection; it continued through the ascension and the session, and it continues now.

Working through 1 Peter 3:22 carefully, Pastor Lyro explains that the right hand is a position of both authority and execution — like Joseph at Pharaoh's right hand or Mordecai at the king's right hand. Prior to this position of session, angels, authorities, and powers were subjected to Christ — a subjection accomplished at the cross and confirmed at the resurrection. The ascension is therefore a coronation parade in which the conquering king displays his victory and distributes gifts to his people, specifically the teaching offices of the church (Ephesians 4:11-12). By virtue of union with Christ, believers have ascended with Him and now dwell positionally in the heavenly places — which is the basis for Christian ethics.

In His session, Christ is actively pouring out the Holy Spirit, forgiving the sins of His people, interceding for them before the Father, and working to preserve them to the end. This results in three practical applications: believers are equipped to conduct spiritual warfare under Christ's authority, are free from the fear of demonic powers, and are empowered to make disciples of all nations through the Great Commission — because all authority in heaven and earth belongs to the One who is their Head.
transcript:
## Introduction: Why We Call It the "Session" of Christ

And to turn once again to 1 Peter chapter 3. We're going to consider one verse — 1 Peter 3:22. But first we'll read again verses 18 through 22 together.

"For Christ also suffered once for sins, the just for the unjust, that He might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh but made alive by the Spirit, by whom also He went and preached to the spirits in prison, who formerly were disobedient when once the divine longsuffering waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was being prepared, in which a few, that is, eight souls, were saved through water. There is also an antitype which now saves us — baptism — not the removal of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God through the resurrection of Jesus Christ, who has gone into heaven and is at the right hand of God, angels and authorities and powers having been made subject to Him."

This is the Word of our Lord. Father, we pray that you would open our eyes to see glorious things concerning You from this passage, help us to grow in love with the Lord Jesus Christ. In Jesus's name, amen.

Have you ever wondered why we call the collection of elders of a local church "the session"? The word session means seating, because that's all the elders do — they just sit around and do nothing. No, that's not why. The word session is used for the gathering of the elders because when the elders are seated together, they are seated together to rule the church, and they are in session at that point.

That's why it is appropriate to talk about the session of Jesus Christ — the fact that He is seated, enthroned in heaven, ruling over all creation. That's exactly what Jesus is doing right now. In theology, this word "session" is used to describe the ministry of Jesus right now as He is seated, enthroned in heaven, ruling over all things for the church.

1 Peter 3:22 speaks of the ascension and the session of Jesus Christ. We tend to talk often about the life, the death, and the resurrection of Jesus Christ, and we should, because they are of the utmost importance for the Christian faith. But the work of Christ did not really end until He ascended into heaven and was seated at the right hand of the Father.

Now in God's providence, today is Ascension Sunday. This past Thursday was the fortieth day since Easter, since the resurrection. I did not plan this — it just happened that this is the passage we are considering today. I'm not that good of a planner that somehow we landed in 1 Peter 3:22 on Ascension Sunday.

What I want to do is take a look at 1 Peter 3:22, how it works together, then answer the question: what does it mean that Christ ascended? Then answer the question: what does it mean that Christ is in session? And lastly, how does that cause us to live differently?

## What 1 Peter 3:22 Says

Peter says that Jesus is at — literally "in" — the right hand of God. The New Testament authors often use the word "God" to refer to the Father. We might read here and think that Peter is lowering the status of Christ as being somehow non-God, because it says that Jesus Christ is at the right hand of God. But Peter is not doing that — he is just using "God" to refer to the Father. In fact, Peter attributes to Jesus divine characteristics as the ruler of all things that belongs to God alone.

Now the right hand is a position of authority and execution — not just authority, but also execution. Remember the story of Joseph being at the right hand of Pharaoh, which means that Joseph had the same authority and the power to execute as Pharaoh did. We have the example of Mordecai at the right hand of Ahasuerus. That is where our Savior is — in the position of the one who is going to execute the plans of the King.

And this right hand of God is in heaven. Peter says that Jesus first went to heaven and then is now at the right hand of God. The going up to heaven is what we call the ascension. But it is not so much that He is going up, as that He is going up to a throne. This word "ascension" is often used of the crowning of a king, because usually the throne is on a higher level than where the people are. So you ascend onto the throne.

And this heaven where Jesus is at the right hand of God is a created space, because Jesus is there bodily — He is human, and the right hand of God is created space. It is the space where God condescends and makes Himself known to us.

Prior to His being at the right hand of God, the verse tells us, angels, authorities, and powers were made subject to Jesus. Notice it says, "Who has gone into heaven and is at the right hand of God, angels and authorities and powers having been made subject to Him." The action of going to heaven and the subjection happening are grammatically prior to His being at the right hand. In other New Testament passages, the subjection of all powers to Jesus took place at His death. Paul in Colossians 2:15 speaking of the crucifixion: "Having disarmed principalities and powers, He made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them in it." And Hebrews 2:14-15: "That through death He might destroy him who had the power of death, that is, the devil, and release those who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage."

So at the cross, angels and authorities and powers were subjected to Christ — though the cross seems like a place of defeat. The cross is always validated by the resurrection. As Paul says in Ephesians 1:19-23: "What is the exceeding greatness of His power toward us who believe, according to the working of His mighty power which He worked in Christ when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His right hand in the heavenly places, far above all principality and power and might and dominion, and every name that is named... and He put all things under His feet and gave Him to be head over all things to the church which is His body."

Who are the angels, authorities, and powers that Peter lists? I think they represent the entire spiritual world, both good and evil. There is nothing over which Jesus does not have authority. As one theologian said, there is no rogue molecule in creation. Every last molecule, every spiritual being, every physical being, submits to Jesus Christ.

When Peter writes this, he seems to have as background Psalm 2 and Psalm 110. As a matter of fact, all the ascension passages in the New Testament seem to be referring back to Psalm 2 and Psalm 110. In Psalm 110:1: "The Lord said to my Lord, 'Sit at My right hand till I make Your enemies Your footstool.'" This is the most quoted verse in the New Testament, and Jesus even specifically applies it to Himself in conversation with the Pharisees, stumping them with the question in Matthew 22. And Jesus Himself says in Matthew 26:64: "Hereafter you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of the Power and coming on the clouds of heaven."

## What Christ Accomplished in His Ascension

What did Christ accomplish in His ascension? First, Christ's ascension declares His victory over all His enemies. In Ephesians 4:7-8, quoting Psalm 68: "Therefore He says: 'When He ascended on high, He led captivity captive, and gave gifts to men.'" This is the picture of a conquering king: His ascension is that parade where He is showing to all the people that He has conquered His enemies, and as He goes, He is also distributing gifts to the people as He goes. That is the picture Paul has in mind for the ascension of our Lord.

I grew up in Brazil under a military dictatorship. So every September 7th — it was completely voluntary, and if you didn't attend, you would be in trouble — you had to attend the independence parade in which the generals would put their troops before you and show who had the power and keep you in subjection. All the might of the Brazilian army was displayed to keep the citizenry under control of the military. Well, that's kind of what Jesus did — He is demonstrating by His ascension that He is the General who conquered all the enemies. But He's not just a conquering General — He's a gifting General. As He ascended, He gave gifts to the church.

You remember what the gifts are? From Ephesians 4:11-12: the teaching offices of the church — apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, teachers. Our conquering King, as He went in His coronation parade, gave as symbols of His authority to His church the teaching offices that equip the church to grow in Jesus Christ.

He also brought His people into the heavenly places. As He ascended, we — because of our union with Him — were brought into His presence in the heavenly places. That's why Colossians 3 can say: "Since you have been raised with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ is, sitting at the right hand of God." And Ephesians 2:4-7 also teaches this. It is not inaccurate to say that you and I dwell in heaven even today. And that's the basis for our ethics in this life — we live heavenly lives because we dwell in heaven with the Lord Jesus Christ.

And Christ's ascension foreshadows our future ascension and rule with Him. Throughout the Scriptures, our Savior tells us that His church will ascend into the throne with Him and rule with Him.

## What Christ Is Doing in His Session

Have you ever considered the importance of this teaching? The New Testament is full of references to Christ's session.

In Matthew 26:64, Jesus says: "Hereafter you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of the Power." Acts 2:33-34, Peter preaching: "Therefore being exalted to the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, He poured out this which you now see and hear." So what is Christ doing in His session? He is pouring out the Holy Spirit on His church.

Acts 5:31: "Him God has exalted to His right hand to be Prince and Savior, to give repentance to Israel and forgiveness of sins." What is Christ doing as the King seated on the throne? He is forgiving the sins of His people — like the illustration in Matthew 18, where the king forgives the person with the great debt. That is our Lord doing in His session.

Romans 8:34: "Who is he who condemns? It is Christ who died, and furthermore is also risen, who is even at the right hand of God, who also makes intercession for us." Someone comes as it were to God the Father and says, "You should let this person go" — and Christ says, "No. I died for him. I intercede for him." And because of that, there is nothing that can separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus, because Christ is seated in heaven on the throne making intercession.

Hebrews 12:2: "Looking unto Jesus, the author and finisher of our faith, who for the joy that was set before Him endured the cross, despising the shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God." Christ in His session is working on us that we might persevere to the end. He is doing that for us.

One commentator said that Christ's session is an indication of Christ's present reversal of authority, the finality of His complete work of redemption, and His immeasurable worthiness to receive praise. That is your Savior, Church of Jesus Christ. He is immeasurably worthy to receive our praise.

## How We Are to Live in Light of the Session of Christ

Paul teaches that we have not only been raised with Christ and ascended with Him to the heavenly places, but also even now Christians have a share in the spiritual authority which belongs to Jesus. This authority finds expression in our lives in three areas.

First, **we are equipped to conduct spiritual warfare.** In 2 Corinthians 10 we are supposed to tear down the strongholds of the enemy by bringing every thought captive to Jesus Christ. In Ephesians 6, we are to put on the whole armor of God that we might engage in the battle that is before us. How do you live out the truth that Christ is seated on the throne? You exercise the power given you by His Spirit to conduct spiritual warfare.

Second, **we have freedom from fearing demonic powers.** You and I as believers in the Lord Jesus Christ do not have to fear the devil. We are to resist him — we are to put on the armor of God — but we don't fear him. James says to submit to God, resist the devil, and what happens? He flees from us. And 1 Peter 5:8-9 says: "Be sober, be vigilant, because your adversary the devil walks about like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour. Resist him, steadfast in the faith." Why? Because Christ is Lord even over the devil, and we are seated with Him in the heavenly places.

Third, **we exercise dominion over the created order through the power of the gospel.** How do we exercise that dominion? You go and you make disciples of all nations. We can do that because all authority belongs to Jesus and He is ruling in heaven for us. Brothers and sisters, Jesus Christ is risen but He is also ascended into heaven and is ruling over all things for His church. And we are united with Him. So be of a heavenly mind so that you can be of earthly good — making disciples of all nations under the authority of Jesus Christ so that every nation will bow its knees to Jesus Christ.

Let us pray together. Father in heaven, thank you that You are God who reveals to us that Your Son rules over all things and was given by You to us to be our Head. Enable us to live under His authority, His leadership, His ministry, that we might bring the world to Jesus Christ. We thank You, Father, that the Lord Jesus Christ is ascended. We thank You that He is our Head, and we pray all these things in Jesus's name. Amen.
